  target_user_sid = if event_data.TargetUserSid != null {
    to_string(event_data.TargetUserSid) ?? null
  } else if event_data.TargetSid != null {
    to_string(event_data.TargetSid) ?? null
  } else {
    null
  }
  parent_process_name = to_string(event_data.ParentProcessName) ?? null
  command_line = to_string(event_data.CommandLine) ?? null

  .class_uid    = class_uid
  .category_uid = category_uid
  .activity_id  = activity_id
  .severity_id  = ocsf_severity
  .time         = ts
  .status       = status
  .status_id    = status_id

  .actor = {
    "user": {
      "name":   subject_user,
      "domain": to_string(event_data.SubjectDomainName) ?? null
    }
  }

  .user = {
    "name":   target_user,
    "domain": target_domain,
    "uid":    target_user_sid
  }

  .src_endpoint = {
    "ip":       to_string(event_data.IpAddress) ?? null,
    "port":     to_int(event_data.IpPort) ?? null,
    "hostname": computer
  }

  # Windows LogonType (2=Interactive, 3=Network...) maps to OCSF
  # logon_type_id — NOT auth_protocol_id (1=NTLM, 2=Kerberos...).
  .logon_type_id = logon_type

  .process = {
    "name":     process_name,
    "cmd_line": command_line,
    "parent_process": {
      "name": parent_process_name
    }
  }

  .metadata = {
    "product": {
      "name":        "Windows Security",
      "vendor_name": "Microsoft"
    },
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "uid":     to_string(event_id)
  }

  .unmapped = {
    "event_id":        event_id,
    "logon_type":      logon_type,
    "process_name":    process_name,
    "parent_process":  parent_process_name,
    "command_line":    command_line,
    "logon_process":   to_string(event_data.LogonProcessName) ?? null,
    "failure_reason":  to_string(event_data.FailureReason) ?? null,
    "sub_status":      to_string(event_data.SubStatus) ?? null
  }

  .raw = raw_msg
  .uid = uuid_v4()
  del(.message)
  del(.winlog)
  del(.event_data)
  del(.timestamp)
  del(.computer_name)
  del(.event_id)
  del(.source_type)
'''

# --- FILTER : drop unknown events ---
[transforms.winevtx_filter_valid]
type      = "filter"
inputs    = ["winevtx_parse_and_normalize"]
condition = '.class_uid != 0'

# --- ROUTE : dispatch par class_uid vers l'index OCSF correspondant ---
[transforms.winevtx_route_by_class]
type   = "route"
inputs = ["winevtx_filter_valid"]
route.identity = '.class_uid == 3001 || .class_uid == 3002'
route.endpoint = '.class_uid == 1007'

[transforms.winevtx_filter_failed]
type      = "filter"
inputs    = ["winevtx_parse_and_normalize"]
condition = '.class_uid == 0'

[transforms.winevtx_raw_failed]
type   = "remap"
inputs = ["winevtx_filter_failed"]
source = '''
  . = {
    "uid":           .uid,
    "time":          .time,
    "received_time": .received_time,
    "tenant_id":     .tenant_id,
    "datasource_id": .datasource_id,
    "source_type":   "identity/windows-security-evtx",
    "parser":        "windows-security-evtx",
    "parse_status":  "failed",
    "parse_error":   "windows_security_event_id_unsupported",
    "raw":           .raw
  }
'''

# --- SINK : Quickwit ocsf-identity (auth + account) ---
[sinks.winevtx_quickwit_identity]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["winevtx_route_by_class.identity"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/ocsf-identity/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.winevtx_quickwit_identity.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

# --- SINK : Quickwit ocsf-endpoint (process 1007: 4688/4698) ---
[sinks.winevtx_quickwit_endpoint]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["winevtx_route_by_class.endpoint"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/ocsf-endpoint/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.winevtx_quickwit_endpoint.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

# --- SINK : Quickwit raw-logs (echecs de parsing uniquement) ---
[sinks.winevtx_quickwit_raw_failed]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["winevtx_raw_failed"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/raw-logs/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.winevtx_quickwit_raw_failed.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

parsers.description: Collecte les evenements Sysmon via Winlogbeat ou NXLog (format JSON). Normalise en OCSF selon le type d'event : - Event 1 (Process Create) → classe 1007 (Process Activity) - Event 7 (Image/Module Loaded) → classe 1005 (Module Activity) - Event 3 (Network Connection) → classe 4001 (Network Activity) - Event 22 (DNS Query) → classe 4003 (DNS Activity)
parsers.vector_toml:
# ==============================================================================
# Windows Sysmon — Events 1/3/7/22
# OCSF Class : 1007 (Process Activity) / 1005 (Module Activity)
#              4001 (Network Activity) / 4003 (DNS Activity)
# Input      : HTTP (JSON from Winlogbeat/NXLog)
# Tested with: Sysmon 15.x, Windows 10/11/Server 2022
# ==============================================================================

# --- SOURCE ---
[sources.sysmon_input]
type     = "http_server"
address  = "0.0.0.0:${LISTEN_PORT:-8515}"
encoding = "json"

# --- TRANSFORM : parse + normalisation OCSF ---
[transforms.sysmon_parse_and_normalize]
type   = "remap"
inputs = ["sysmon_input"]
source = '''
  raw_msg       = encode_json(.)
  tenant_id     = "${TENANT_ID}"
  datasource_id = "${DATASOURCE_ID}"
  received_time = to_unix_timestamp(now(), unit: "milliseconds")

  # Sysmon via Winlogbeat (.winlog.*) or NXLog (top level). Explicit null
  # checks: to_int(null)=0 / to_string(null)="" are infallible, so a `??`
  # chain never reaches the top-level fallback — it must be an if/else.
  event_id = 0
  if .winlog.event_id != null {
    event_id = to_int(.winlog.event_id) ?? 0
  } else if .event_id != null {
    event_id = to_int(.event_id) ?? 0
  }
  event_data = object(.winlog.event_data) ?? object(.event_data) ?? {}
  computer = "unknown"
  if .winlog.computer_name != null {
    computer = to_string(.winlog.computer_name) ?? "unknown"
  } else if .computer_name != null {
    computer = to_string(.computer_name) ?? "unknown"
  }

  # Event time, not transport time. Prefer Sysmon's own UtcTime
  # ("YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.sss", UTC), then Winlogbeat's @timestamp (RFC3339),
  # and only fall back to the http_server ingest timestamp when both absent.
  event_time_str = to_string(event_data.UtcTime) ?? null
  atstamp_str    = to_string(get(., ["@timestamp"]) ?? null) ?? null
  parsed_time = null
  if event_time_str != null {
    parsed_time = parse_timestamp(event_time_str, format: "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S%.f") ?? parse_timestamp(event_time_str, format: "%+") ?? null
  } else if atstamp_str != null {
    parsed_time = parse_timestamp(atstamp_str, format: "%+") ?? null
  }
  if parsed_time == null {
    ingest_str = to_string(.timestamp) ?? null
    if ingest_str != null {
      parsed_time = parse_timestamp(ingest_str, format: "%+") ?? null
    }
  }
  ts = if parsed_time != null { to_unix_timestamp!(parsed_time, unit: "milliseconds") } else { received_time }

  # Classification (D1/D6):
  # 1  = Process Create     → 1007 Process Activity   (category 1)
  # 7  = Image/Module Loaded → 1005 Module Activity    (category 1)
  # 3  = Network Connection → 4001 Network Activity    (category 4)
  # 22 = DNS Query          → 4003 DNS Activity        (category 4)
  is_process = event_id == 1
  is_module  = event_id == 7
  is_network = event_id == 3
  is_dns     = event_id == 22

  class_uid = if is_process { 1007 } else if is_module { 1005 } else if is_network { 4001 } else if is_dns { 4003 } else { 0 }
  category_uid = if class_uid != 0 { to_int(floor(class_uid / 1000)) } else { 0 }

  # activity_id per class (D6). Sysmon EventID is fixed per event type, so
  # the activity is known: process-create=Launch, module-load=Other (Sysmon
  # never reports unload), network=Traffic, DNS=Query.
  activity_id = if is_process { 1 } else if is_module { 99 } else if is_network { 6 } else if is_dns { 1 } else { 0 }

  ocsf_severity = 1

  # Sysmon Hashes is "SHA256=<hex>,MD5=<hex>,...". Extract the SHA256 value.
  hashes_str  = to_string(event_data.Hashes) ?? null
  sha256_match = if hashes_str != null { parse_regex(hashes_str, r'SHA256=(?P<value>[0-9A-Fa-f]+)') ?? null } else { null }
  sha256_value = if sha256_match != null { sha256_match.value } else { null }

  query_results = to_string(event_data.QueryResults) ?? null
  qtype_match   = if query_results != null { parse_regex(query_results, r'type:\s*(?P<type>\d+)') ?? null } else { null }
  query_type    = if qtype_match != null { qtype_match.type } else { null }

  metadata = {
    "product": {
      "name":        "Sysmon",
      "vendor_name": "Microsoft"
    },
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "uid":     to_string(event_id)
  }

  unmapped = {
    "event_id":      event_id,
    "rule_name":     to_string(event_data.RuleName) ?? null,
    "guid":          to_string(event_data.ProcessGuid) ?? null,
    "query_results": query_results
  }

  # Rebuild the document from scratch — only OCSF fields. The winlogbeat
  # envelope (agent, ecs, host, @timestamp, .path, .message) never leaks.
  . = {
    "class_uid":     class_uid,
    "category_uid":  category_uid,
    "severity_id":   ocsf_severity,
    "activity_id":   activity_id,
    "time":          ts,
    "received_time": received_time,
    "tenant_id":     tenant_id,
    "datasource_id": datasource_id,
    "metadata":      metadata,
    "unmapped":      unmapped,
    "raw":           raw_msg,
    "uid":           uuid_v4()
  }

  # Per-class substructures — attached only to the class that owns them, never
  # null-filled objects on the wrong event type.
  if is_process {
    .actor = {
      "user": { "name": to_string(event_data.User) ?? null },
      "process": {
        "pid":      to_int(event_data.ParentProcessId) ?? null,
        "name":     to_string(event_data.ParentImage) ?? null,
        "cmd_line": to_string(event_data.ParentCommandLine) ?? null
      }
    }
    .process = {
      "pid":      to_int(event_data.ProcessId) ?? null,
      "name":     to_string(event_data.Image) ?? null,
      "cmd_line": to_string(event_data.CommandLine) ?? null,
      "file": {
        "hashes": [
          { "algorithm": "SHA256", "value": sha256_value }
        ]
      }
    }
  }

  if is_module {
    .actor = {
      "process": {
        "pid":  to_int(event_data.ProcessId) ?? null,
        "name": to_string(event_data.Image) ?? null
      }
    }
    .module = {
      "name": to_string(event_data.ImageLoaded) ?? null,
      "hashes": [
        { "algorithm": "SHA256", "value": sha256_value }
      ]
    }
  }

  if is_network {
    .src_endpoint = {
      "ip":       to_string(event_data.SourceIp) ?? null,
      "port":     to_int(event_data.SourcePort) ?? null,
      "hostname": computer
    }
    .dst_endpoint = {
      "ip":       to_string(event_data.DestinationIp) ?? null,
      "port":     to_int(event_data.DestinationPort) ?? null,
      "hostname": to_string(event_data.DestinationHostname) ?? null
    }
  }

  if is_dns {
    .query = {
      "hostname": to_string(event_data.QueryName) ?? null,
      "type":     query_type
    }
  }
'''

# --- FILTER ---
[transforms.sysmon_filter_valid]
type      = "filter"
inputs    = ["sysmon_parse_and_normalize"]
condition = '.class_uid != 0'

# --- ROUTE : dispatch par class_uid ---
[transforms.sysmon_route_by_class]
type   = "route"
inputs = ["sysmon_filter_valid"]
route.endpoint = '.class_uid == 1007 || .class_uid == 1005'
route.network  = '.class_uid == 4001'
route.dns      = '.class_uid == 4003'

[transforms.sysmon_filter_failed]
type      = "filter"
inputs    = ["sysmon_parse_and_normalize"]
condition = '.class_uid == 0'

[transforms.sysmon_raw_failed]
type   = "remap"
inputs = ["sysmon_filter_failed"]
source = '''
  . = {
    "uid":           .uid,
    "time":          .time,
    "received_time": .received_time,
    "tenant_id":     .tenant_id,
    "datasource_id": .datasource_id,
    "source_type":   "identity/windows-sysmon",
    "parser":        "windows-sysmon",
    "parse_status":  "failed",
    "parse_error":   "sysmon_event_id_unsupported",
    "raw":           .raw
  }
'''

# --- SINK : Quickwit ocsf-endpoint ---
[sinks.sysmon_quickwit_endpoint]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["sysmon_route_by_class.endpoint"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/ocsf-endpoint/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.sysmon_quickwit_endpoint.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

# --- SINK : Quickwit ocsf-network ---
[sinks.sysmon_quickwit_network]

batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.sysmon_quickwit_network.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

# --- SINK : Quickwit ocsf-dns ---
[sinks.sysmon_quickwit_dns]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["sysmon_route_by_class.dns"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/ocsf-dns/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.sysmon_quickwit_dns.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

# --- SINK : Quickwit raw-logs (echecs de parsing uniquement) ---
[sinks.sysmon_quickwit_raw_failed]

parsers.description: Collecte les logs du daemon d'audit Linux (`auditd`). Normalise en OCSF : - SYSCALL/EXECVE/PROCTITLE → classe 1003 (Process Activity) - USER_AUTH/USER_LOGIN/CRED_ACQ → classe 3002 (Authentication)
parsers.vector_toml:
# ==============================================================================
# Linux auditd — SYSCALL, EXECVE, PROCTITLE, USER_AUTH, USER_LOGIN
# OCSF Class : 1007 (Process Activity) + 3002 (Authentication)
# Input      : Syslog TCP (rsyslog forwarde /var/log/audit/audit.log via imfile)
# Tested with: auditd 3.0+, Linux 5.x/6.x
# rsyslog   : imfile /var/log/audit/audit.log -> omfwd target port LISTEN_PORT TCP
# ==============================================================================

# --- SOURCE ---
[sources.auditd_input]
type    = "syslog"
address = "0.0.0.0:${LISTEN_PORT:-5143}"
mode    = "tcp"

# --- TRANSFORM : parse + OCSF normalization ---
[transforms.auditd_parse_and_normalize]
type   = "remap"
inputs = ["auditd_input"]
source = '''
  .tenant_id     = "${TENANT_ID}"
  .datasource_id = "${DATASOURCE_ID}"
  .received_time = to_unix_timestamp(now(), unit: "milliseconds")

  # Parse auditd key=value format
  # type=SYSCALL msg=audit(1713091425.123:456): arch=c000003e syscall=59 ...
  raw_msg = string!(.message)

  # Extract event type
  type_match = parse_regex(raw_msg, r'^type=(?P<type>[A-Z_]+)') ?? {}
  audit_type = type_match.type

  # Extract timestamp from msg=audit(EPOCH.MS:SERIAL)
  ts_match = parse_regex(raw_msg, r'msg=audit\((?P<epoch>\d+)\.(?P<ms>\d+):(?P<serial>\d+)\)') ?? {}
  epoch_s = ts_match.epoch
  epoch_ms_raw = ts_match.ms

  # Compute timestamp in milliseconds
  if epoch_s != null {
    .time = to_int!(epoch_s) * 1000 + to_int!(epoch_ms_raw)
  } else {
    .time = to_unix_timestamp!(now(), unit: "milliseconds")
  }

  # Parse all key=value pairs after the header
  kv_part = replace(raw_msg, r'^type=[A-Z_]+ msg=audit\([^)]+\): ?', "")
  # USER_* records nest their fields inside msg='op=... res=...':
  # parse_key_value treats the single quote as a quoting char and swallows
  # the whole block as one value (res/hostname/addr become unreachable).
  # Stripping the quotes promotes the inner pairs to the top level.
  kv_part = replace(kv_part, "'", "")
  pairs = parse_key_value(kv_part, key_value_delimiter: "=", field_delimiter: " ") ?? {}

  # Determine OCSF class from auditd type
  is_auth = audit_type == "USER_AUTH" || audit_type == "USER_LOGIN" || audit_type == "CRED_ACQ" || audit_type == "USER_ACCT"
  is_process = audit_type == "SYSCALL" || audit_type == "EXECVE" || audit_type == "PROCTITLE"
  valid = is_auth || is_process
  class_uid = if is_auth { 3002 } else { 1007 }
  category_uid = if is_auth { 3 } else { 1 }

  # activity_id : auth (3002) → 1 Logon ; process (1007) → 1 Launch
  activity = if is_auth { "Logon" } else { "Launch" }
  activity_id = if is_auth { 1 } else { 1 }

  # Severity : SYSCALL success=yes → info, success=no → medium
  success = pairs.success
  res = pairs.res
  ocsf_severity = if success == "no" || res == "failed" { 3 } else { 1 }

  if valid {
    .class_uid     = class_uid
    .category_uid  = category_uid
    .severity_id   = ocsf_severity
    .activity_name = activity
    .activity_id   = activity_id
  } else {
    .class_uid    = 0
    .category_uid = 0
    .severity_id  = 0
  }

  # Process info (SYSCALL/EXECVE)
  uid_val = pairs.uid
  auid_val = pairs.auid
  pid_raw = pairs.pid
  pid_val = if pid_raw != null { to_int(pid_raw) ?? null } else { null }
  comm_val = pairs.comm
  proctitle_val = pairs.proctitle

  .actor = {
    "user": {
      "uid":  uid_val,
      "name": auid_val
    },
    "process": {
      "pid":  pid_val,
      "name": comm_val,
      "cmd_line": proctitle_val
    }
  }

  hostname_val = pairs.hostname
  addr_val = pairs.addr

  .src_endpoint = {
    "hostname": hostname_val,
    "ip":       addr_val
  }

  serial_val = ts_match.serial

  .metadata = {
    "product": {
      "name":        "Linux auditd",
      "vendor_name": "Linux"
    },
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "uid":     serial_val
  }

  syscall_val = pairs.syscall
  arch_val = pairs.arch
  key_val = pairs.key

  .unmapped = {
    "audit_type": audit_type,
    "syscall":    syscall_val,
    "arch":       arch_val,
    "key":        key_val
  }

  # Full raw line (syslog header + auditd content) + unique identifier
  _ts   = to_string(.timestamp) ?? ""
  _host = string(.hostname)     ?? ""
  _app  = string(.appname)      ?? ""
  .raw  = _ts + " " + _host + " " + _app + ": " + raw_msg
  .uid  = uuid_v4()

  del(.message)
  del(.timestamp)
  del(.hostname)
  del(.appname)
  del(.facility)
  del(.severity)
  del(.procid)
  del(.msgid)
  del(.version)
  del(.source_type)
'''

# --- FILTER : drop empty ---
[transforms.auditd_filter_valid]
type      = "filter"
inputs    = ["auditd_parse_and_normalize"]
condition = '.class_uid == 1007 || .class_uid == 3002'

# --- ROUTE : dispatch by class_uid (3002 USER_AUTH → identity, 1007 SYSCALL → endpoint) ---
[transforms.auditd_route_by_class]
type   = "route"
inputs = ["auditd_filter_valid"]
route.identity = '.class_uid == 3002'
route.endpoint = '.class_uid == 1001 || .class_uid == 1007'

[transforms.auditd_filter_failed]
type      = "filter"
inputs    = ["auditd_parse_and_normalize"]
condition = '.class_uid != 1007 && .class_uid != 3002'

# --- TRANSFORM : extract failures for raw-logs ---
[transforms.auditd_raw_only]
type   = "remap"
inputs = ["auditd_filter_failed"]
source = '''
  # A runtime abort in the normalize transform forwards the ORIGINAL event here
  # (drop_on_error defaults to false): every field must be re-derived locally.
  raw_val = .raw
  if raw_val == null { raw_val = .message }
  if raw_val == null { raw_val = encode_json(.) }
  now_ms = to_unix_timestamp(now(), unit: "milliseconds")
  uid_val = .uid
  if uid_val == null { uid_val = uuid_v4() }
  time_val = .time
  if time_val == null { time_val = now_ms }
  received_val = .received_time
  if received_val == null { received_val = now_ms }
  . = {
    "uid":           uid_val,
    "time":          time_val,
    "received_time": received_val,
    "tenant_id":     "${TENANT_ID}",
    "datasource_id": "${DATASOURCE_ID}",
    "source_type":   "linux/auditd",
    "parser":        "auditd",
    "parse_status":  "failed",
    "parse_error":   "auditd_type_unsupported",
    "raw":           raw_val
  }
'''

# --- SINK : Quickwit ocsf-endpoint (SYSCALL/EXECVE) ---
[sinks.auditd_quickwit_endpoint]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["auditd_route_by_class.endpoint"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/ocsf-endpoint/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.auditd_quickwit_endpoint.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

# --- SINK : Quickwit ocsf-identity (USER_AUTH/USER_LOGIN/CRED_ACQ/USER_ACCT) ---
[sinks.auditd_quickwit_identity]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["auditd_route_by_class.identity"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/ocsf-identity/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.auditd_quickwit_identity.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

# --- SINK : Quickwit raw-logs ---
[sinks.auditd_quickwit_raw]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["auditd_raw_only"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/raw-logs/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.auditd_quickwit_raw.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

parsers.description: Collecte les evenements d'authentification Linux depuis `/var/log/auth.log`. Normalise en OCSF classe 3002 (Authentication).
parsers.vector_toml:
# ==============================================================================
# Linux auth.log — SSH, sudo, PAM authentication events
# OCSF Class : 3002 (Authentication)
# Input      : Syslog TCP (rsyslog forwarde auth,authpriv facility)
# Tested with: OpenSSH 8.x/9.x, sudo 1.9.x, Ubuntu/Debian
# rsyslog   : auth,authpriv.* -> omfwd target port LISTEN_PORT protocol TCP
# ==============================================================================

# --- SOURCE ---
[sources.authlog_input]
type    = "syslog"
address = "0.0.0.0:${LISTEN_PORT:-5142}"
mode    = "tcp"

# --- TRANSFORM : parse + OCSF normalization ---
[transforms.authlog_parse_and_normalize]
type   = "remap"
inputs = ["authlog_input"]
source = '''
  .tenant_id     = "${TENANT_ID}"
  .datasource_id = "${DATASOURCE_ID}"
  .received_time = to_unix_timestamp(now(), unit: "milliseconds")

  # Vector syslog source parses the header and provides:
  # .hostname, .appname, .procid, .timestamp, .message (message body only)
  hostname = string(.hostname) ?? ""
  app      = string(.appname) ?? ""
  pid_raw  = .procid
  pid      = if pid_raw != null { to_int(pid_raw) ?? null } else { null }
  msg      = string!(.message)
  raw_msg  = msg

  # Determine event type and extract details from the message body
  is_ssh_accept = match(msg, r'Accepted\s+(password|publickey)\s+for')
  is_ssh_fail = match(msg, r'Failed\s+(password|publickey)\s+for')
  is_ssh_invalid = match(msg, r'Invalid user')
  is_sudo = app == "sudo" || match(msg, r'sudo:')
  is_pam = match(msg, r'pam_unix\(')
  valid = is_ssh_accept || is_ssh_fail || is_ssh_invalid || is_sudo || is_pam

  # SSH accepted: "Accepted publickey for user from 1.2.3.4 port 54321 ssh2"
  ssh_accept = parse_regex(msg, r'Accepted\s+(?P<method>\S+)\s+for\s+(?P<user>\S+)\s+from\s+(?P<ip>\S+)\s+port\s+(?P<port>\d+)') ?? {}

  # SSH failed: "Failed password for user from 1.2.3.4 port 54321 ssh2"
  # SSH failed invalid: "Failed password for invalid user admin from 1.2.3.4 port 54321 ssh2"
  ssh_fail = parse_regex(msg, r'Failed\s+(?P<method>\S+)\s+for\s+(?:invalid user\s+)?(?P<user>\S+)\s+from\s+(?P<ip>\S+)\s+port\s+(?P<port>\d+)') ?? {}

  # sudo: "user : TTY=pts/0 ; PWD=/home/user ; USER=root ; COMMAND=/usr/bin/apt update"
  sudo_match = parse_regex(msg, r'(?P<user>\S+)\s+:\s+TTY=(?P<tty>\S+)\s+;\s+PWD=(?P<pwd>\S+)\s+;\s+USER=(?P<target_user>\S+)\s+;\s+COMMAND=(?P<command>.+)$') ?? {}

  # Build fields based on event type (ssh_fail/sudo_match if ssh_accept is empty).
  # In VRL, `??` only falls back on *errors* from fallible expressions, not on `null`
  # (a null value is "infallible") — here regex captures return null => explicit cascade.
  user_name = if ssh_accept.user != null { ssh_accept.user } else if ssh_fail.user != null { ssh_fail.user } else if sudo_match.user != null { sudo_match.user } else { null }
  src_ip = if ssh_accept.ip != null { ssh_accept.ip } else if ssh_fail.ip != null { ssh_fail.ip } else { null }
  src_port_raw = if ssh_accept.port != null { ssh_accept.port } else if ssh_fail.port != null { ssh_fail.port } else { null }
  src_port = if src_port_raw != null { to_int(src_port_raw) ?? null } else { null }
  auth_method = if ssh_accept.method != null { ssh_accept.method } else if ssh_fail.method != null { ssh_fail.method } else { null }

  # Severity and status
  is_success = is_ssh_accept || (is_sudo && !match(msg, r'authentication failure'))
  is_failure = is_ssh_fail || is_ssh_invalid || match(msg, r'authentication failure')
  ocsf_severity = if is_failure { 3 } else { 1 }

  status = if is_success { "Success" } else if is_failure { "Failure" } else { "Other" }
  status_id = if is_success { 1 } else if is_failure { 2 } else { 99 }

  # SSH login → 1 (Logon). sudo privilege escalation has no slot in the
  # Authentication 3002 activity_id enum (1 Logon..7 Account Switch) → 99 (Other).
  activity = if is_ssh_accept || is_ssh_fail || is_ssh_invalid { "Logon" } else if is_sudo { "Other" } else { "Other" }
  activity_id = if is_ssh_accept || is_ssh_fail || is_ssh_invalid { 1 } else if is_sudo { 99 } else { 99 }

  if valid {
    .class_uid     = 3002
    .category_uid  = 3
    .severity_id   = ocsf_severity
    .status        = status
    .status_id     = status_id
    .activity_name = activity
    .activity_id   = activity_id
  } else {
    .class_uid    = 0
    .category_uid = 0
    .severity_id  = 0
  }
  .time = to_unix_timestamp(.timestamp, unit: "milliseconds") ?? to_unix_timestamp(now(), unit: "milliseconds")

  .actor = {
    "user": {
      "name": user_name
    },
    "process": {
      "name": app,
      "pid":  pid
    }
  }

  .src_endpoint = {
    "ip":       src_ip,
    "port":     src_port,
    "hostname": hostname
  }

  .auth_protocol = auth_method

  .metadata = {
    "product": {
      "name":        "Linux auth.log",
      "vendor_name": "Linux"
    },
    "version": "1.0.0"
  }

  sudo_cmd = sudo_match.command
  sudo_target = sudo_match.target_user

  .unmapped = {
    "sudo_command":     sudo_cmd,
    "sudo_target_user": sudo_target
  }

  # Full raw line (syslog header + message) + unique identifier
  _pid = if pid_raw != null { "[" + (to_string(pid_raw) ?? "") + "]" } else { "" }
  _ts  = to_string(.timestamp) ?? ""
  .raw = _ts + " " + hostname + " " + app + _pid + ": " + raw_msg
  .uid = uuid_v4()

  del(.message)
  del(.timestamp)
  del(.hostname)
  del(.appname)
  del(.facility)
  del(.severity)
  del(.procid)
  del(.msgid)
  del(.version)
  del(.source_type)
'''

# --- FILTER : drop empty ---
[transforms.authlog_filter_valid]
type      = "filter"
inputs    = ["authlog_parse_and_normalize"]
condition = '.class_uid == 3002'

[transforms.authlog_filter_failed]
type      = "filter"
inputs    = ["authlog_parse_and_normalize"]
condition = '.class_uid != 3002'

# --- TRANSFORM : extract failures for raw-logs ---
[transforms.authlog_raw_only]
type   = "remap"
inputs = ["authlog_filter_failed"]
source = '''
  # A runtime abort in the normalize transform forwards the ORIGINAL event here
  # (drop_on_error defaults to false): every field must be re-derived locally.
  raw_val = .raw
  if raw_val == null { raw_val = .message }
  if raw_val == null { raw_val = encode_json(.) }
  now_ms = to_unix_timestamp(now(), unit: "milliseconds")
  uid_val = .uid
  if uid_val == null { uid_val = uuid_v4() }
  time_val = .time
  if time_val == null { time_val = now_ms }
  received_val = .received_time
  if received_val == null { received_val = now_ms }
  . = {
    "uid":           uid_val,
    "time":          time_val,
    "received_time": received_val,
    "tenant_id":     "${TENANT_ID}",
    "datasource_id": "${DATASOURCE_ID}",
    "source_type":   "linux/auth-log",
    "parser":        "auth-log",
    "parse_status":  "failed",
    "parse_error":   "auth_log_pattern_unmatched",
    "raw":           raw_val
  }
'''

# --- SINK : Quickwit OCSF ---
[sinks.authlog_quickwit]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["authlog_filter_valid"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/ocsf-identity/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.authlog_quickwit.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

# --- SINK : Quickwit raw-logs ---
[sinks.authlog_quickwit_raw]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["authlog_raw_only"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/raw-logs/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.authlog_quickwit_raw.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

parsers.description: Collects Postfix mail activity from `maillog` (the `mail` syslog facility) and normalises it to OCSF class 4009 (Email Activity). The collective routes mail events through the same syslog block as the other Linux sources.
parsers.vector_toml:
# ==============================================================================
# Postfix maillog — SMTP delivery, queue, reject events
# OCSF Class : 4009 (Email Activity)
# Input      : Syslog TCP (rsyslog forwards mail facility)
# Tested with: Postfix 3.x, Ubuntu/Debian
# rsyslog   : mail.* -> omfwd target port LISTEN_PORT protocol TCP
# Routing   : OCSF 4009 has no dedicated email index — routed to ocsf-network.
# ==============================================================================

# --- SOURCE ---
[sources.postfix_input]
type    = "syslog"
address = "0.0.0.0:${LISTEN_PORT:-5146}"
mode    = "tcp"

# --- TRANSFORM : parse + OCSF normalization ---
[transforms.postfix_parse_and_normalize]
type   = "remap"
inputs = ["postfix_input"]
source = '''
  .tenant_id     = "${TENANT_ID}"
  .datasource_id = "${DATASOURCE_ID}"
  .received_time = to_unix_timestamp(now(), unit: "milliseconds")

  # Vector syslog source parses the header and provides:
  # .hostname, .appname, .procid, .timestamp, .message (message body only)
  hostname = string(.hostname) ?? ""
  app      = string(.appname) ?? ""
  pid_raw  = .procid
  msg      = string!(.message)
  raw_msg  = msg

  # Postfix subprograms log via a "subprog[pid]:" prefix that the syslog
  # source keeps in the body. Strip it so the regexes match the payload.
  body = replace(msg, r'^\w+\[\d+\]:\s*', "")

  # --- Event-type detection on the message body ---
  is_connect = match(body, r'^connect from ')
  is_reject  = match(body, r'reject:')
  is_qmgr    = match(body, r'from=<[^>]*>,\s*size=')
  is_smtp    = match(body, r'\bto=<[^>]*>') && match(body, r'\bstatus=')
  valid = is_connect || is_reject || is_qmgr || is_smtp

  # connect: "connect from mail.example.com[1.2.3.4]"
  connect_match = parse_regex(body, r'connect from (?P<client_host>[^\[\s]+)\[(?P<client_ip>[0-9a-fA-F:.]+)\]') ?? {}

  # qmgr from line: "A1B2C3: from=<sender@example.com>, size=1024, nrcpt=1 (queue active)"
  qmgr_match = parse_regex(body, r'from=<(?P<from>[^>]*)>,\s*size=(?P<size>\d+)') ?? {}

  # smtp delivery: "A1B2C3: to=<rcpt@example.org>, relay=mx.example.org[5.6.7.8]:25, delay=0.5, status=sent (250 2.0.0 OK)"
  smtp_match = parse_regex(body, r'to=<(?P<to>[^>]*)>(?:,\s*relay=(?P<relay>[^,\s]+))?.*?status=(?P<status>\w+)') ?? {}

  # reject: "NOQUEUE: reject: RCPT from unknown[9.9.9.9]: 554 5.7.1 Service unavailable; from=<x@y.com> to=<z@w.com>"
  reject_client = parse_regex(body, r'reject:\s+\w+\s+from\s+(?P<client_host>[^\[\s]+)\[(?P<client_ip>[0-9a-fA-F:.]+)\]') ?? {}
  reject_from   = parse_regex(body, r'from=<(?P<from>[^>]*)>') ?? {}
  reject_to     = parse_regex(body, r'to=<(?P<to>[^>]*)>') ?? {}

  # --- Field extraction (explicit cascade — `??` only falls back on errors,
  # not on null returned by an unmatched regex capture) ---
  email_from = if qmgr_match.from != null { qmgr_match.from } else if reject_from.from != null { reject_from.from } else { null }
  email_to   = if smtp_match.to != null { smtp_match.to } else if reject_to.to != null { reject_to.to } else { null }
  email_size_raw = qmgr_match.size
  email_size = if email_size_raw != null { to_int(email_size_raw) ?? null } else { null }
  smtp_status = if smtp_match.status != null { smtp_match.status } else { null }
  relay_host  = if smtp_match.relay != null { smtp_match.relay } else { null }
  client_host = if connect_match.client_host != null { connect_match.client_host } else if reject_client.client_host != null { reject_client.client_host } else { null }
  client_ip   = if connect_match.client_ip != null { connect_match.client_ip } else if reject_client.client_ip != null { reject_client.client_ip } else { null }

  # --- Severity & activity ---
  # reject/bounce → Medium (3), deferred → Low (2), sent/connect → Info (1).
  is_deferred = smtp_status == "deferred"
  is_bounced  = smtp_status == "bounced"
  ocsf_severity = if is_reject || is_bounced { 3 } else if is_deferred { 2 } else { 1 }

  # OCSF 4009 (Email Activity) has no activity_id entry in the validator enum
  # table — pick sensible integers: send/sent=1, receive/connect=2, reject=99.
  activity_id = if is_reject { 99 } else if is_connect { 2 } else { 1 }

  if valid {
    .class_uid    = 4009
    .category_uid = 4
    .severity_id  = ocsf_severity
    .activity_id  = activity_id
  } else {
    .class_uid    = 0
    .category_uid = 0
    .severity_id  = 0
  }
  .time = to_unix_timestamp(.timestamp, unit: "milliseconds") ?? to_unix_timestamp(now(), unit: "milliseconds")

  .email = {
    "from": email_from,
    "to":   email_to,
    "size": email_size
  }

  .smtp_status = smtp_status
  .relay       = relay_host

  .src_endpoint = {
    "ip":       client_ip,
    "hostname": client_host
  }

  .metadata = {
    "product": {
      "name":        "Postfix",
      "vendor_name": "Postfix"
    },
    "version": "1.0.0"
  }

  # Full raw line (syslog header + message) + unique identifier
  _pid = if pid_raw != null { "[" + (to_string(pid_raw) ?? "") + "]" } else { "" }
  _ts  = to_string(.timestamp) ?? ""
  .raw = _ts + " " + hostname + " " + app + _pid + ": " + raw_msg
  .uid = uuid_v4()

  del(.message)
  del(.timestamp)
  del(.hostname)
  del(.appname)
  del(.facility)
  del(.severity)
  del(.procid)
  del(.msgid)
  del(.version)
  del(.source_type)
'''

# --- FILTER : valid OCSF events ---
[transforms.postfix_filter_valid]
type      = "filter"
inputs    = ["postfix_parse_and_normalize"]
condition = '.class_uid == 4009'

[transforms.postfix_filter_failed]
type      = "filter"
inputs    = ["postfix_parse_and_normalize"]
condition = '.class_uid != 4009'

# --- TRANSFORM : extract failures for raw-logs ---
[transforms.postfix_raw_only]
type   = "remap"
inputs = ["postfix_filter_failed"]
source = '''
  # A runtime abort in the normalize transform forwards the ORIGINAL event here
  # (drop_on_error defaults to false): every field must be re-derived locally.
  raw_val = .raw
  if raw_val == null { raw_val = .message }
  if raw_val == null { raw_val = encode_json(.) }
  now_ms = to_unix_timestamp(now(), unit: "milliseconds")
  uid_val = .uid
  if uid_val == null { uid_val = uuid_v4() }
  time_val = .time
  if time_val == null { time_val = now_ms }
  received_val = .received_time
  if received_val == null { received_val = now_ms }
  . = {
    "uid":           uid_val,
    "time":          time_val,
    "received_time": received_val,
    "tenant_id":     "${TENANT_ID}",
    "datasource_id": "${DATASOURCE_ID}",
    "source_type":   "linux/postfix",
    "parser":        "postfix",
    "parse_status":  "failed",
    "parse_error":   "postfix_pattern_unmatched",
    "raw":           raw_val
  }
'''

# --- SINK : Quickwit OCSF (4009 routed to ocsf-network, no dedicated email index) ---
[sinks.postfix_quickwit]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["postfix_filter_valid"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/ocsf-network/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.postfix_quickwit.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

# --- SINK : Quickwit raw-logs ---
[sinks.postfix_quickwit_raw]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["postfix_raw_only"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/raw-logs/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.postfix_quickwit_raw.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

parsers.description: Collecte les logs syslog Linux (rsyslog, syslog-ng, systemd-journald) via TCP/UDP. Une ligne syslog generique ne porte aucune activite OCSF intrinseque : elle est emise en OCSF Base Event (class_uid 0 / category_uid 0, decret D2) avec `parse_status = "parsed"` et routee vers l'index `raw-logs`. Parser de repli : les parsers specifiques (auth-log, parsers de service) sont preferes quand la source est connue.
parsers.vector_toml:
# ==============================================================================
# Linux Syslog — RFC 3164 / RFC 5424
# OCSF Class : 0 (Base Event) — a generic syslog line carries no intrinsic
#              OCSF activity (decree D2). Fallback parser: app-specific parsers
#              (auth-log, service parsers) are preferred when the source is known.
# Input      : Syslog TCP/UDP
# Tested with: rsyslog, syslog-ng, systemd-journald
# ==============================================================================

# --- SOURCE ---
[sources.syslog_input]
type    = "syslog"
address = "0.0.0.0:${LISTEN_PORT:-5141}"
mode    = "tcp"

# --- TRANSFORM : parse + OCSF normalization ---
[transforms.syslog_parse_and_normalize]
type   = "remap"
inputs = ["syslog_input"]
source = '''
  .tenant_id     = "${TENANT_ID}"
  .datasource_id = "${DATASOURCE_ID}"
  .received_time = to_unix_timestamp(now(), unit: "milliseconds")

  # Vector syslog source automatically parses:
  # .timestamp, .hostname, .appname, .facility, .severity, .message
  # Normalize to OCSF

  # .severity can be an integer (RFC 3164) or a string (RFC 5424 via rsyslog).
  # RFC 5424 names: emerg/alert/crit/err/warning/notice/info/debug (+ common synonyms).
  sev_raw = .severity
  sev = if is_integer(sev_raw) {
    to_int!(sev_raw)
  } else {
    s = downcase(to_string(sev_raw) ?? "info")
    if s == "emerg" || s == "emergency" || s == "panic" { 0 } else if s == "alert" { 1 } else if s == "crit" || s == "critical" { 2 } else if s == "err" || s == "error" { 3 } else if s == "warning" || s == "warn" { 4 } else if s == "notice" { 5 } else if s == "info" || s == "informational" { 6 } else if s == "debug" { 7 } else { 6 }
  }
  ocsf_severity = if sev <= 2 { 5 } else if sev <= 3 { 4 } else if sev <= 4 { 3 } else if sev <= 5 { 2 } else { 1 }

  facility = .facility
  procid_val = .procid

  # Decree D2: a generic syslog line carries no intrinsic OCSF activity.
  # Emit a Base Event (class 0 / category 0) — no activity_id on Base Event.
  .class_uid    = 0
  .category_uid = 0
  .severity_id  = ocsf_severity
  .time         = to_unix_timestamp(.timestamp, unit: "milliseconds") ?? to_unix_timestamp(now(), unit: "milliseconds")
  .parse_status = "parsed"

  .metadata = {
    "product": {
      "name":        "Linux Syslog",
      "vendor_name": "Linux"
    },
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "log_provider": facility
  }

  # Full raw line (syslog header + message) + unique identifier
  _ts  = to_string(.timestamp) ?? ""
  _pid = if procid_val != null { "[" + (to_string(procid_val) ?? "") + "]" } else { "" }
  _msg = to_string(.message)   ?? ""
  .raw = _ts + " " + (string(.hostname) ?? "") + " " + (string(.appname) ?? "") + _pid + ": " + _msg
  .uid = uuid_v4()

  # Clean up native syslog fields
  del(.message)
  del(.timestamp)
  del(.hostname)
  del(.appname)
  del(.facility)
  del(.severity)
  del(.procid)
  del(.msgid)
  del(.version)
  del(.source_type)
'''

# --- FILTER : drop empty ---
[transforms.syslog_filter_valid]
type      = "filter"
inputs    = ["syslog_parse_and_normalize"]
condition = '.parse_status == "parsed" && .raw != null && .raw != ""'

[transforms.syslog_filter_failed]
type      = "filter"
inputs    = ["syslog_parse_and_normalize"]
condition = '.parse_status != "parsed" || .raw == null || .raw == ""'

# --- TRANSFORM : extract failures for raw-logs ---
[transforms.syslog_raw_only]
type   = "remap"
inputs = ["syslog_filter_failed"]
source = '''
  # A runtime abort in the normalize transform forwards the ORIGINAL event here
  # (drop_on_error defaults to false): every field must be re-derived locally.
  raw_val = .raw
  if raw_val == null { raw_val = .message }
  if raw_val == null { raw_val = encode_json(.) }
  now_ms = to_unix_timestamp(now(), unit: "milliseconds")
  uid_val = .uid
  if uid_val == null { uid_val = uuid_v4() }
  time_val = .time
  if time_val == null { time_val = now_ms }
  received_val = .received_time
  if received_val == null { received_val = now_ms }
  . = {
    "uid":           uid_val,
    "time":          time_val,
    "received_time": received_val,
    "tenant_id":     "${TENANT_ID}",
    "datasource_id": "${DATASOURCE_ID}",
    "source_type":   "linux/syslog",
    "parser":        "syslog",
    "parse_status":  "failed",
    "parse_error":   "syslog_empty_raw",
    "raw":           raw_val
  }
'''

# --- SINK : Quickwit raw-logs (parsed Base Event — decree D2) ---
[sinks.syslog_quickwit]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["syslog_filter_valid"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/raw-logs/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.syslog_quickwit.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

# --- SINK : Quickwit raw-logs ---
[sinks.syslog_quickwit_raw]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["syslog_raw_only"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/raw-logs/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.syslog_quickwit_raw.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

parsers.description: Collecte les logs FortiGate (format key=value) via syslog. Normalise en OCSF classe 4001 (Network Activity).
parsers.vector_toml:
# ==============================================================================
# Fortinet FortiGate — traffic/utm logs (key=value format)
# OCSF Class : 4001 (Network Activity)
# Input      : Syslog TCP/UDP
# Tested with: FortiOS 7.2.x / 7.4.x
# ==============================================================================

# --- SOURCE ---
[sources.fortigate_input]
type    = "syslog"
address = "0.0.0.0:${LISTEN_PORT:-5144}"
mode    = "tcp"

# --- TRANSFORM : parse + normalisation OCSF ---
[transforms.fortigate_parse_and_normalize]
type   = "remap"
inputs = ["fortigate_input"]
source = '''
  .tenant_id     = "${TENANT_ID}"
  .datasource_id = "${DATASOURCE_ID}"
  .received_time = to_unix_timestamp(now(), unit: "milliseconds")

  raw_msg = string!(.message)

  # FortiGate log format : key=value key="quoted value" ...
  # date=2024-04-14 time=10:23:45 devname="FG100F" devid="FG100FXXXXXXXXXX"
  # logid="0000000013" type="traffic" subtype="forward" level="notice"
  # srcip=10.0.0.5 srcport=54321 dstip=8.8.8.8 dstport=443 ...
  kv = parse_key_value(raw_msg, key_value_delimiter: "=", field_delimiter: " ") ?? {}

  fgt_type = kv.type
  fgt_subtype = kv.subtype
  fgt_action = kv.action
  fgt_level = kv.level

  # Valider qu'on a bien un log FortiGate (null != "" est true en VRL, utiliser is_string)
  valid = is_string(fgt_type) && fgt_type != ""

  if valid {
    # Timestamp : priorite au header syslog (timezone fiable du daemon).
    # Le payload FortiGate "date=... time=..." n'embarque pas de TZ — le device
    # envoie en heure locale et supposer UTC decale l'horloge.
    .time = to_unix_timestamp(.timestamp, unit: "milliseconds") ?? to_unix_timestamp(now(), unit: "milliseconds")

    # Action OCSF
    ocsf_action = if fgt_action == "accept" || fgt_action == "allow" || fgt_action == "passthrough" {
      "Allow"
    } else if fgt_action == "deny" || fgt_action == "drop" || fgt_action == "blocked" {
      "Deny"
    } else if fgt_action == "close" || fgt_action == "timeout" {
      "Close"
    } else {
      "Other"
    }
    ocsf_action_id = if ocsf_action == "Allow" { 1 } else if ocsf_action == "Deny" { 2 } else if ocsf_action == "Close" { 3 } else { 0 }

    # OCSF 4001 activity_id (D6): {1 Open, 2 Close, 3 Reset, 4 Fail, 5 Refuse,
    # 6 Traffic, 7 Listen, 99 Other}. FortiGate traffic logs describe a flow →
    # 6 (Traffic); a teardown (deny/drop/blocked/close/timeout) → 2 (Close).
    ocsf_activity_id = if fgt_action == "deny" || fgt_action == "drop" || fgt_action == "blocked" || fgt_action == "close" || fgt_action == "timeout" {
      2
    } else {
      6
    }

    # Severity mapping : emergency/alert/critical → 5, error → 4, warning → 3, notice → 2, info → 1
    ocsf_severity = if fgt_level == "emergency" || fgt_level == "alert" || fgt_level == "critical" {
      5
    } else if fgt_level == "error" {
      4
    } else if fgt_level == "warning" {
      3
    } else if fgt_level == "notice" {
      2
    } else {
      1
    }

    .class_uid    = 4001
    .category_uid = 4
    .severity_id  = ocsf_severity
    .activity_id  = ocsf_activity_id
    .action       = ocsf_action
    .action_id    = ocsf_action_id

    srcip = kv.srcip
    srcport_raw = kv.srcport
    srcport = if srcport_raw != null { to_int(srcport_raw) ?? null } else { null }
    dstip = kv.dstip
    dstport_raw = kv.dstport
    dstport = if dstport_raw != null { to_int(dstport_raw) ?? null } else { null }

    .src_endpoint = {
      "ip":   srcip,
      "port": srcport
    }
    .dst_endpoint = {
      "ip":   dstip,
      "port": dstport
    }

    proto_raw = kv.proto
    proto_num = if proto_raw != null { to_int(proto_raw) ?? null } else { null }
    service_name = kv.service
    direction_val = kv.direction

    .connection_info = {
      "protocol_num": proto_num,
      "protocol_name": service_name,
      "direction":     direction_val
    }

    rcvdbyte_raw = kv.rcvdbyte
    sentbyte_raw = kv.sentbyte
    rcvdpkt_raw = kv.rcvdpkt
    sentpkt_raw = kv.sentpkt
    bytes_in = if rcvdbyte_raw != null { to_int(rcvdbyte_raw) ?? null } else { null }
    bytes_out = if sentbyte_raw != null { to_int(sentbyte_raw) ?? null } else { null }
    packets_in = if rcvdpkt_raw != null { to_int(rcvdpkt_raw) ?? null } else { null }
    packets_out = if sentpkt_raw != null { to_int(sentpkt_raw) ?? null } else { null }

    .traffic = {
      "bytes_in":    bytes_in,
      "bytes_out":   bytes_out,
      "packets_in":  packets_in,
      "packets_out": packets_out
    }

    logid_val = kv.logid

    .metadata = {
      "product": {
        "name":        "FortiGate",
        "vendor_name": "Fortinet"
      },
      "version": "1.0.0",
      "uid":     logid_val
    }

    policyid_val = kv.policyid
    devname_val = kv.devname
    srcintf_val = kv.srcintf
    dstintf_val = kv.dstintf
    duration_raw = kv.duration
    duration_val = if duration_raw != null { to_int(duration_raw) ?? null } else { null }
    appcat_val = kv.appcat
    app_val = kv.app

    .unmapped = {
      "type":     fgt_type,
      "subtype":  fgt_subtype,
      "policyid": policyid_val,
      "devname":  devname_val,
      "srcintf":  srcintf_val,
      "dstintf":  dstintf_val,
      "duration": duration_val,
      "appcat":   appcat_val,
      "app":      app_val
    }
  } else {
    .class_uid    = 0
    .category_uid = 0
    .severity_id  = 0
    .time         = to_unix_timestamp!(now(), unit: "milliseconds")
  }

  .raw = del(.message)
  .uid = uuid_v4()
  del(.timestamp)
  del(.hostname)
  del(.appname)
  del(.facility)
  del(.severity)
  del(.procid)
  del(.msgid)
  del(.version)
  del(.source_type)
'''

# --- FILTER : drop malformed ---
[transforms.fortigate_filter_valid]
type      = "filter"
inputs    = ["fortigate_parse_and_normalize"]
condition = '.class_uid == 4001'

[transforms.fortigate_filter_failed]
type      = "filter"
inputs    = ["fortigate_parse_and_normalize"]
condition = '.class_uid != 4001'

[transforms.fortigate_raw_failed]
type   = "remap"
inputs = ["fortigate_filter_failed"]
source = '''
  # A runtime abort in the normalize transform forwards the ORIGINAL event here
  # (drop_on_error defaults to false): every field must be re-derived locally.
  raw_val = .raw
  if raw_val == null { raw_val = .message }
  if raw_val == null { raw_val = encode_json(.) }
  now_ms = to_unix_timestamp(now(), unit: "milliseconds")
  uid_val = .uid
  if uid_val == null { uid_val = uuid_v4() }
  time_val = .time
  if time_val == null { time_val = now_ms }
  received_val = .received_time
  if received_val == null { received_val = now_ms }
  . = {
    "uid":           uid_val,
    "time":          time_val,
    "received_time": received_val,
    "tenant_id":     "${TENANT_ID}",
    "datasource_id": "${DATASOURCE_ID}",
    "source_type":   "network/fortinet-fortigate",
    "parser":        "fortigate",
    "parse_status":  "failed",
    "parse_error":   "fortigate_key_value_invalid",
    "raw":           raw_val
  }
'''

# --- SINK : Quickwit ---
[sinks.fortigate_quickwit]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["fortigate_filter_valid"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/ocsf-network/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.fortigate_quickwit.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

# --- SINK : Quickwit raw-logs (echecs de parsing uniquement) ---
[sinks.fortigate_quickwit_raw_failed]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["fortigate_raw_failed"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/raw-logs/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.fortigate_quickwit_raw_failed.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

parsers.description: Collecte les logs filterlog d'OPNsense via syslog. Normalise en OCSF classe 4001 (Network Activity).
parsers.vector_toml:
# ==============================================================================
# OPNsense Firewall — filterlog
# OCSF Class : 4001 (Network Activity)
# Input      : Syslog TCP/UDP
# Tested with: OPNsense 24.x/25.x
# ==============================================================================

# --- SOURCE ---
[sources.opnsense_input]
type    = "syslog"
address = "0.0.0.0:${LISTEN_PORT:-5140}"
mode    = "tcp"

# --- TRANSFORM : parse + OCSF normalization ---
[transforms.opnsense_parse_and_normalize]
type   = "remap"
inputs = ["opnsense_input"]
source = '''
  .tenant_id     = "${TENANT_ID}"
  .datasource_id = "${DATASOURCE_ID}"
  .received_time = to_unix_timestamp(now(), unit: "milliseconds")

  raw_msg = string!(.message)

  # filterlog CSV format (OPNsense / pfSense) — layout differs by IP family:
  # 0=rulenr, 1=subrulenr, 2=anchorname, 3=label, 4=interface, 5=reason, 6=action,
  # 7=direction, 8=ip_version
  # IPv4 (ip_version=4):
  #   9=tos, 10=ecn, 11=ttl, 12=id, 13=offset, 14=flags,
  #   15=proto_id, 16=proto, 17=length, 18=src_ip, 19=dst_ip
  #   TCP/UDP: 20=src_port, 21=dst_port
  # IPv6 (ip_version=6):
  #   9=class, 10=flow-label, 11=hop_limit, 12=proto, 13=proto_id, 14=length,
  #   15=src_ip, 16=dst_ip
  #   TCP/UDP: 17=src_port, 18=dst_port

  fields = split(raw_msg, ",")
  field_count = length(fields)

  # Need at least 17 fields (IPv6 ICMPv6 without port: 17 is sufficient)
  valid = field_count >= 17

  if valid {
    action_raw = fields[6]
    direction = fields[7]
    ip_version = fields[8]
    interface = fields[4]

    is_v6 = ip_version == "6"
    # VRL: array indexing only accepts literals; branch on is_v6 accordingly.
    proto_num_raw = if is_v6 { fields[13] } else { fields[15] }
    proto = if is_v6 { fields[12] } else { fields[16] }
    src_ip = if is_v6 { fields[15] } else { fields[18] }
    dst_ip = if is_v6 { fields[16] } else { fields[19] }
    src_port_str = if is_v6 {
      if field_count > 17 { fields[17] } else { "" }
    } else {
      if field_count > 20 { fields[20] } else { "" }
    }
    dst_port_str = if is_v6 {
      if field_count > 18 { fields[18] } else { "" }
    } else {
      if field_count > 21 { fields[21] } else { "" }
    }

    src_port = if src_port_str != "" { to_int(src_port_str) ?? null } else { null }
    dst_port = if dst_port_str != "" { to_int(dst_port_str) ?? null } else { null }
    proto_num = if proto_num_raw != null { to_int(proto_num_raw) ?? null } else { null }

    ocsf_action = if action_raw == "pass" { "Allow" } else { "Deny" }
    ocsf_action_id = if action_raw == "pass" { 1 } else { 2 }
    ocsf_severity = if action_raw == "pass" { 1 } else { 2 }

    # OCSF 4001 activity_id (D6): {1 Open, 2 Close, 3 Reset, 4 Fail, 5 Refuse,
    # 6 Traffic, 7 Listen, 99 Other}. A filterlog line (pass or block) records
    # an observed packet/flow → 6 (Traffic).
    ocsf_activity_id = 6

    .class_uid    = 4001
    .category_uid = 4
    .severity_id  = ocsf_severity
    .activity_id  = ocsf_activity_id
    .time         = to_unix_timestamp(.timestamp, unit: "milliseconds") ?? to_unix_timestamp(now(), unit: "milliseconds")
    .action       = ocsf_action
    .action_id    = ocsf_action_id

    .src_endpoint = {
      "ip":   src_ip,
      "port": src_port
    }
    .dst_endpoint = {
      "ip":   dst_ip,
      "port": dst_port
    }
    .connection_info = {
      "protocol_num": proto_num,
      "protocol_name": proto,
      "direction":     direction
    }

    .metadata = {
      "product": {
        "name":        "OPNsense",
        "vendor_name": "OPNsense Project"
      },
      "version": "1.0.0"
    }

    rule_nr = fields[0]

    .unmapped = {
      "interface":  interface,
      "rule_nr":    rule_nr,
      "ip_version": ip_version
    }
  } else {
    .class_uid    = 0
    .category_uid = 0
    .severity_id  = 0
    .time         = to_unix_timestamp!(now(), unit: "milliseconds")
  }

  # Full raw line (syslog header + filterlog CSV) + unique identifier
  _ts   = to_string(.timestamp) ?? ""
  _host = string(.hostname)     ?? ""
  _app  = string(.appname)      ?? ""
  .raw  = _ts + " " + _host + " " + _app + ": " + raw_msg
  .uid  = uuid_v4()

  del(.message)
  del(.timestamp)
  del(.hostname)
  del(.appname)
  del(.facility)
  del(.severity)
  del(.procid)
  del(.msgid)
  del(.version)
  del(.source_type)
'''

# --- FILTER : drop malformed ---
[transforms.opnsense_filter_valid]
type      = "filter"
inputs    = ["opnsense_parse_and_normalize"]
condition = '.class_uid == 4001'

[transforms.opnsense_filter_failed]
type      = "filter"
inputs    = ["opnsense_parse_and_normalize"]
condition = '.class_uid != 4001'

# --- TRANSFORM : extract failures for raw-logs ---
[transforms.opnsense_raw_only]
type   = "remap"
inputs = ["opnsense_filter_failed"]
source = '''
  . = {
    "uid":           .uid,
    "time":          .time,
    "received_time": .received_time,
    "tenant_id":     .tenant_id,
    "datasource_id": .datasource_id,
    "source_type":   "network/opnsense",
    "parser":        "opnsense",
    "parse_status":  "failed",
    "parse_error":   "filterlog_csv_invalid",
    "raw":           .raw
  }
'''

# --- SINK : Quickwit OCSF ---
[sinks.opnsense_quickwit]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["opnsense_filter_valid"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/ocsf-network/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.opnsense_quickwit.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

# --- SINK : Quickwit raw-logs ---
[sinks.opnsense_quickwit_raw]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["opnsense_raw_only"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/raw-logs/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.opnsense_quickwit_raw.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

parsers.description: Collecte les evenements Suricata EVE JSON depuis un fichier `eve.json`. Le parser route dynamiquement : - `alert` → OCSF 2001 Security Finding (`ocsf-endpoint`) - `flow`, `netflow`, `tls` → OCSF 4001 Network Activity (`ocsf-network`) - `http` → OCSF 4002 HTTP Activity (`ocsf-http`) - `dns` → OCSF 4003 DNS Activity (`ocsf-dns`)
parsers.vector_toml:
# ==============================================================================
# Suricata — EVE JSON
# OCSF Classes: 2001 (Security Finding), 4001 (Network), 4002 (HTTP), 4003 (DNS)
# Input       : File (JSON per line)
# Tested with : Suricata 7.x EVE JSON
# ==============================================================================

# --- SOURCE ---
[sources.suricata_input]
type    = "file"
include = ["${SURICATA_EVE_LOG:-/var/log/suricata/eve.json}"]

# --- TRANSFORM : parse + normalisation OCSF ---
[transforms.suricata_parse_and_normalize]
type   = "remap"
inputs = ["suricata_input"]
source = '''
  .tenant_id     = "${TENANT_ID}"
  .datasource_id = "${DATASOURCE_ID}"
  .received_time = to_unix_timestamp(now(), unit: "milliseconds")

  raw_msg = string!(.message)
  parsed = parse_json(raw_msg) ?? null
  valid_json = is_object(parsed)

  if valid_json {
    ev = object!(parsed)

    event_type = to_string(get(ev, ["event_type"]) ?? "") ?? ""
    valid = event_type == "alert" || event_type == "dns" || event_type == "http" || event_type == "flow" || event_type == "netflow" || event_type == "tls"

    ts_str = to_string(get(ev, ["timestamp"]) ?? "") ?? ""
    parsed_ts = parse_timestamp(ts_str, format: "%+") ?? parse_timestamp(ts_str, format: "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%.f%z") ?? null
    ts = if parsed_ts != null { to_unix_timestamp!(parsed_ts, unit: "milliseconds") } else { to_unix_timestamp!(now(), unit: "milliseconds") }

    src_ip = to_string(get(ev, ["src_ip"]) ?? null) ?? null
    dest_ip = to_string(get(ev, ["dest_ip"]) ?? null) ?? null
    src_port_raw = get(ev, ["src_port"]) ?? null
    dest_port_raw = get(ev, ["dest_port"]) ?? null
    src_port = if src_port_raw != null { to_int(src_port_raw) ?? null } else { null }
    dest_port = if dest_port_raw != null { to_int(dest_port_raw) ?? null } else { null }
    proto = to_string(get(ev, ["proto"]) ?? null) ?? null
    app_proto = to_string(get(ev, ["app_proto"]) ?? null) ?? null
    flow_id = to_string(get(ev, ["flow_id"]) ?? null) ?? null

    alert = object(get(ev, ["alert"]) ?? {}) ?? {}
    dns = object(get(ev, ["dns"]) ?? {}) ?? {}
    http = object(get(ev, ["http"]) ?? {}) ?? {}
    tls = object(get(ev, ["tls"]) ?? {}) ?? {}
    flow = object(get(ev, ["flow"]) ?? {}) ?? {}

    alert_sev_raw = get(alert, ["severity"]) ?? null
    alert_sev = if alert_sev_raw != null { to_int(alert_sev_raw) ?? null } else { null }
    ocsf_severity = if event_type == "alert" {
      if alert_sev == 1 { 4 } else if alert_sev == 2 { 3 } else if alert_sev == 3 { 2 } else { 1 }
    } else {
      1
    }

    class_uid = if !valid { 0 } else if event_type == "alert" { 2001 } else if event_type == "dns" { 4003 } else if event_type == "http" { 4002 } else { 4001 }
    category_uid = if class_uid == 2001 { 2 } else if class_uid == 0 { 0 } else { 4 }

    # activity_id per OCSF class (pre-computed: no if in object literal).
    # DNS 4003   : query->1, answer/response->2 (Suricata dns.type)
    # HTTP 4002  : mapped from http.http_method
    # Net 4001   : flow/netflow/tls observations -> 6 (Traffic)
    # Finding 2001: a Suricata alert is a generated finding -> 1 (Generate)
    dns_type = downcase(to_string(get(dns, ["type"]) ?? "") ?? "")
    method_upper = upcase(to_string(get(http, ["http_method"]) ?? "") ?? "")
    http_activity_id = if method_upper == "GET" { 3 } else if method_upper == "POST" { 6 } else if method_upper == "PUT" { 7 } else if method_upper == "DELETE" { 2 } else if method_upper == "HEAD" { 4 } else if method_upper == "OPTIONS" { 5 } else if method_upper == "PATCH" { 9 } else if method_upper == "CONNECT" { 1 } else if method_upper == "TRACE" { 8 } else { 99 }
    dns_activity_id = if dns_type == "query" { 1 } else if dns_type == "answer" || dns_type == "response" { 2 } else { 99 }
    activity_id = if class_uid == 4003 { dns_activity_id } else if class_uid == 4002 { http_activity_id } else if class_uid == 4001 { 6 } else if class_uid == 2001 { 1 } else { 0 }

    .class_uid    = class_uid
    .category_uid = category_uid
    .severity_id  = if class_uid == 0 { 0 } else { ocsf_severity }
    .activity_id  = activity_id
    .time         = ts

    .src_endpoint = {
      "ip":   src_ip,
      "port": src_port
    }

    .dst_endpoint = {
      "ip":   dest_ip,
      "port": dest_port
    }

    .connection_info = {
      "protocol_name": proto,
      "app_protocol":  app_proto
    }

    query_hostname = to_string(get(dns, ["rrname"]) ?? null) ?? null
    query_type = to_string(get(dns, ["rrtype"]) ?? null) ?? null
    query_rcode = to_string(get(dns, ["rcode"]) ?? null) ?? null
    dns_answers = get(dns, ["answers"]) ?? null
    .query = {
      "hostname": query_hostname,
      "type":     query_type,
      "rcode":    query_rcode
    }
    .answers = dns_answers

    http_method = to_string(get(http, ["http_method"]) ?? null) ?? null
    http_host = to_string(get(http, ["hostname"]) ?? null) ?? null
    http_url = to_string(get(http, ["url"]) ?? null) ?? null
    http_status_raw = get(http, ["status"]) ?? null
    http_status = if http_status_raw != null { to_int(http_status_raw) ?? null } else { null }
    http_user_agent = to_string(get(http, ["http_user_agent"]) ?? null) ?? null
    http_referrer = to_string(get(http, ["http_refer"]) ?? null) ?? null
    http_protocol = to_string(get(http, ["protocol"]) ?? null) ?? null

    .http_request = {
      "http_method": http_method,
      "url": {
        "hostname": http_host,
        "path":     http_url
      },
      "version":    http_protocol,
      "user_agent": http_user_agent,
      "referrer":   http_referrer
    }

    .http_response = {
      "code": http_status
    }

    alert_signature = to_string(get(alert, ["signature"]) ?? null) ?? null
    alert_signature_id = to_string(get(alert, ["signature_id"]) ?? null) ?? null
    alert_category = to_string(get(alert, ["category"]) ?? null) ?? null
    alert_action = to_string(get(alert, ["action"]) ?? null) ?? null

    .finding_info = {
      "title": alert_signature,
      "uid":   alert_signature_id,
      "types": [alert_category]
    }

    bytes_toserver_raw = get(flow, ["bytes_toserver"]) ?? null
    bytes_toclient_raw = get(flow, ["bytes_toclient"]) ?? null
    pkts_toserver_raw = get(flow, ["pkts_toserver"]) ?? null
    pkts_toclient_raw = get(flow, ["pkts_toclient"]) ?? null
    bytes_out = if bytes_toserver_raw != null { to_int(bytes_toserver_raw) ?? null } else { null }
    bytes_in = if bytes_toclient_raw != null { to_int(bytes_toclient_raw) ?? null } else { null }
    packets_out = if pkts_toserver_raw != null { to_int(pkts_toserver_raw) ?? null } else { null }
    packets_in = if pkts_toclient_raw != null { to_int(pkts_toclient_raw) ?? null } else { null }

    .traffic = {
      "bytes_in":    bytes_in,
      "bytes_out":   bytes_out,
      "packets_in":  packets_in,
      "packets_out": packets_out
    }

    .tls = {
      "sni":     to_string(get(tls, ["sni"]) ?? null) ?? null,
      "version": to_string(get(tls, ["version"]) ?? null) ?? null,
      "issuer":  to_string(get(tls, ["issuerdn"]) ?? null) ?? null,
      "subject": to_string(get(tls, ["subject"]) ?? null) ?? null
    }

    .metadata = {
      "product": {
        "name":        "Suricata",
        "vendor_name": "OISF"
      },
      "version": "1.0.0",
      "uid":     flow_id
    }

    .unmapped = {
      "event_type":   event_type,
      "flow_id":      flow_id,
      "in_iface":     to_string(get(ev, ["in_iface"]) ?? null) ?? null,
      "alert_action": alert_action,
      "alert_gid":    to_string(get(alert, ["gid"]) ?? null) ?? null,
      "alert_rev":    to_string(get(alert, ["rev"]) ?? null) ?? null
    }
  } else {
    .class_uid    = 0
    .category_uid = 0
    .severity_id  = 0
    .time         = to_unix_timestamp!(now(), unit: "milliseconds")
  }

  .raw = raw_msg
  .uid = uuid_v4()

  del(.message)
  del(.file)
  del(.host)
  del(.source_type)
'''

# --- FILTERS / ROUTES ---
[transforms.suricata_filter_valid]
type      = "filter"
inputs    = ["suricata_parse_and_normalize"]
condition = '.class_uid != 0'

[transforms.suricata_filter_failed]
type      = "filter"
inputs    = ["suricata_parse_and_normalize"]
condition = '.class_uid == 0'

[transforms.suricata_route_by_class]
type   = "route"
inputs = ["suricata_filter_valid"]
route.finding = '.class_uid == 2001'
route.network = '.class_uid == 4001'
route.http    = '.class_uid == 4002'
route.dns     = '.class_uid == 4003'

[transforms.suricata_raw_failed]
type   = "remap"
inputs = ["suricata_filter_failed"]
source = '''
  . = {
    "uid":           .uid,
    "time":          .time,
    "received_time": .received_time,
    "tenant_id":     .tenant_id,
    "datasource_id": .datasource_id,
    "source_type":   "network/suricata-eve",
    "parser":        "suricata-eve",
    "parse_status":  "failed",
    "parse_error":   "suricata_eve_json_or_event_type_invalid",
    "raw":           .raw
  }
'''

# --- SINKS : Quickwit OCSF ---
[sinks.suricata_quickwit_finding]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["suricata_route_by_class.finding"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/ocsf-endpoint/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.suricata_quickwit_finding.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

[sinks.suricata_quickwit_network]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["suricata_route_by_class.network"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/ocsf-network/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.suricata_quickwit_network.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

[sinks.suricata_quickwit_http]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["suricata_route_by_class.http"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/ocsf-http/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.suricata_quickwit_http.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

[sinks.suricata_quickwit_dns]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["suricata_route_by_class.dns"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/ocsf-dns/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
batch.timeout_secs  = 5

[sinks.suricata_quickwit_dns.request]
headers.Content-Type = "application/x-ndjson"

# --- SINK : Quickwit raw-logs (echecs de parsing uniquement) ---
[sinks.suricata_quickwit_raw_failed]
type                = "http"
inputs              = ["suricata_raw_failed"]
uri                 = "${QUICKWIT_ENDPOINT}/api/v1/raw-logs/ingest"
encoding.codec      = "json"
framing.method      = "newline_delimited"
batch.max_events    = 1000
